skill.name: ib-summarizer
skill.description: Extract and organize core safety information from Investigator Brochure documents for clinical research and pharmacovigilance.
skill.summary: Automated extraction of adverse reactions, contraindications, and warnings from Investigator Brochures.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After evaluating all 58 static analysis findings, every pattern match is a false positive. The weak cryptographic algorithm alarms are triggered by incidental word occurrences in markdown and JSON files -- this skill performs no cryptography. The shell backtick execution matches are all markdown code blocks showing example command-line usage, not actual shell execution. The system reconnaissance matches are evaluation keywords in the audit result output file. The path traversal matches are security checklist items in SKILL.md and a benign capitalize() call in main.py. The hardcoded URL matches are the legitimate source repository link. The skill is a local document parser with no network calls, no code execution, and no data exfiltration. Minor concern: the code does not validate output file paths against directory traversal, but this is a CLI tool where the user controls all paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 8 locations as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' by matching on incidental words. The code performs no cryptographic operations. Matches occurred on words like 'security' in argparse description strings (main.py:471-472), 'description' fields in markdown frontmatter (SKILL.md:3, README.md:13), and evaluation criteria in the audit result JSON (ib-summarizer_audit_result_v2.json:62). No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC2, RC4, Blowfish, or any crypto library is imported or used.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 37 locations as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' for markdown backtick formatting and code blocks. All matches are in SKILL.md, README.md, and references/audit-reference.md files where backticks denote code syntax highlighting (e.g., ```bash, `pip install`, `scripts/main.py`). No actual shell execution via backticks, os.system, subprocess, exec, or eval exists anywhere in the codebase. scripts/main.py uses only argparse for CLI parsing.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 4 locations in the audit result JSON file as 'system reconnaissance'. These are evaluation keywords in the audit output (ib-summarizer_audit_result_v2.json), not code. No system reconnaissance code exists in the skill: no uname, hostname, ifconfig, netstat, psutil, os.environ, os.listdir, os.walk, or /proc reads are present. The script only reads the user-specified input file and writes to the user-specified output path.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 4 locations as 'path traversal' but none represent actual traversal exploits. scripts/main.py:430 is a capitalize() string method call on a dictionary key, not path manipulation. SKILL.md:223 and SKILL.md:226 are unchecked security checklist items documenting the need for path validation. scripts/main.py:514 writes to a user-specified output path via argparse. No directory traversal sequences are injected into any path operation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a URL in SKILL.md:7 as 'hardcoded URL'. This is the legitimate source repository link pointing to the aipoch/medical-research-skills GitHub repository. No network requests are made to this or any URL in the skill code. scripts/main.py has no import of requests, urllib, socket, or any networking library.

content.user_title: Summarize Drug Safety Data from Investigator Brochures
content.value_statement: Clinical researchers spend hours reading lengthy Investigator Brochures to find core safety information. This skill automatically extracts adverse reactions, contraindications, warnings, and drug interactions from IB documents into structured summaries.
actual_capabilities: Extract known adverse drug reactions with incidence rates and severity levels from IB documents
Identify contraindications, warnings, precautions, and drug interaction information
Parse Investigator Brochures in PDF, DOCX, and plain text formats
Output structured summaries in JSON, Markdown, or plain text
Extract safety information for special populations (pregnancy, pediatric, elderly, hepatic and renal impairment)
Identify overdose symptoms and management guidance from safety sections
limitations: Only extracts information explicitly stated in the source document, cannot infer missing data
Requires well-structured Investigator Brochure documents for best extraction results
Does not perform medical validation or clinical interpretation of extracted data
Limited to text extraction from digital PDF and DOCX files; scanned images need OCR preprocessing

use_cases.title: Clinical Safety Data Review
use_cases.description: A clinical research associate extracts adverse reactions, contraindications, and warnings from multiple Investigator Brochures to prepare a safety overview for review.

use_cases.title: Drug Safety Assessment
use_cases.description: A drug safety physician identifies all adverse drug reactions with incidence rates and severity levels from an IB for safety signal evaluation.

use_cases.title: Regulatory Submission Preparation
use_cases.description: A regulatory writer compiles structured safety information from an IB into Markdown or JSON format for inclusion in regulatory submission documents.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Safety Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract core safety information from this Investigator Brochure. Include drug identity, known adverse reactions with severity, contraindications, and warnings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs a quick overview of a new IB document

prompt_templates.title: Adverse Drug Reactions Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all adverse drug reactions from this IB document. Organize them by body system with incidence rates and severity levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Safety physician reviewing ADR data for safety assessment

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive IB Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract a complete safety profile from this Investigator Brochure. Include all sections: drug identity, adverse reactions, contraindications, warnings, drug interactions, special populations, overdose, and safety updates. Output in Markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regulatory writer needs structured data for submission documents

prompt_templates.title: Custom Output in JSON Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all safety information from this Investigator Brochure and output as structured JSON. Include fields for drug_name, version, adverse_reactions with incidence and severity, contraindications, warnings, and drug_interactions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs programmatic access to IB safety data

output_examples.input: Summarize the adverse reactions from this Investigator Brochure.
output_examples.output: The document lists 5 adverse reactions: headache (45% incidence, mild), nausea (30%, moderate), dizziness (20%, mild), elevated liver enzymes (8%, severe), and rash (5%, moderate). Most reactions resolve without intervention.

output_examples.input: Extract contraindications and warnings from this IB document.
output_examples.output: Contraindications: Hypersensitivity to active substance, concurrent use with MAO inhibitors, severe hepatic impairment. Warnings: Monitor liver function monthly, assess renal function before dosing, discontinue if rash with fever develops.

output_examples.input: Extract safety data for special populations and drug interactions from this IB.
output_examples.output: Pregnancy: Not recommended, limited data available. Elderly: Start at reduced dose, monitor renal function. Hepatic impairment: Contraindicated in severe cases. Drug interactions: CYP3A4 inhibitors increase drug exposure, antacids reduce absorption.

best_practices: Provide the complete Investigator Brochure document for the most comprehensive extraction results.
Specify the output format (JSON, Markdown, or plain text) based on your downstream workflow needs.
Review extracted data against the source document to verify accuracy before using in clinical decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for documents that are not Investigator Brochures, as extraction accuracy will be poor.
Do not rely on extraction results alone for clinical decisions without professional medical review.
Do not provide corrupted or password-protected documents, as they cannot be processed.

faq.question: What types of documents does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports PDF, DOCX, and plain text files containing Investigator Brochure content.

faq.question: What information can this skill extract from an Investigator Brochure?
faq.answer: It extracts drug identity, adverse reactions, contraindications, warnings, drug interactions, special population precautions, overdose information, and safety update history.

faq.question: Does this skill make medical recommendations?
faq.answer: No. This skill only extracts and organizes information from source documents. It does not provide medical advice or clinical interpretation.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Output can be delivered in JSON format for programmatic use, Markdown for documentation, or plain text for simple review.

faq.question: Does this skill send data over the internet?
faq.answer: No. All document processing happens locally. No data is sent over any network.

faq.question: Can this skill process scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: The skill works best with text-based PDFs. Scanned documents require optical character recognition preprocessing before use.
